slides.title: Source-grounded slide generation
slides.subtitle: A fixture deck for the renderer path

visual_aid.purpose: Set a calm opening frame before evidence appears.

speaker_notes.key_message: This deck is about making slide output inspectable before anyone trusts it.
talk_track: Open with the purpose and review posture.
Frame the deck as a test of process, not a polished product demo.
speaker_notes.transition_out: Next, show the pipeline that creates the deck.
delivery_cues: Keep the opening under half a minute.
avoid_saying: Do not promise the system is finished.

slides.title: From raw material to a durable deck
body: Evidence, story, visuals, rendering, critique, audit, QA, export.
Each phase leaves an artifact that can be reviewed.

visual_aid.purpose: Use sparse structure so the audience can orient before details.

speaker_notes.key_message: The workflow separates thinking from rendering.
talk_track: Name the phases at a high level.
Keep this slide short so the audience can follow the rest of the deck.
speaker_notes.transition_in: After the title, the audience needs the map.
speaker_notes.transition_out: Now explain why evidence has to come first.

slides.title: Evidence comes before rendering
body: The story spine and slide sorter shape the argument.
Rendering happens after the deck has a checkable structure.

visual_aid.purpose: Reveal the three steps one at a time so the audience sees order, not clutter.

validation.expected: The HTML deck renders the list as Marp fragments.
validation.fallback: Show the same three bullets as a static list.

motion.purpose: Control pacing for the causal sequence.
motion.reduced_motion_fallback: The full list remains readable without animation.

talk_track: Explain that the workflow is intentionally slower at the beginning.
Connect the slower start to faster, safer repair later.
Make clear that layout changes should not rewrite facts.
speaker_notes.transition_in: The map is useful only if the first step is grounded.
speaker_notes.transition_out: Compare this with the usual prompt-to-deck behavior.
delivery_cues: Advance each bullet only after finishing its sentence.

slides.title: Was: prompt to deck. Is: audited artifacts.

left.title: Was
body: One prompt creates copy, layout, and confidence at the same time.
Review is difficult because the source of each claim is unclear.
A visual fix can accidentally change meaning.

right.title: Is
body: Claims, story, visual aids, and theme are separate artifacts.
Factual statements point back to the ledger.
Repairs target the artifact that failed.

visual_aid.purpose: Make the operating difference visible without adding paragraphs.

validation.expected: Both panels render with clear separation and no text overflow.
validation.fallback: Split the comparison across two slides if the text becomes cramped.

speaker_notes.key_message: The repo exists to make repair cheaper and safer.
talk_track: Contrast a single opaque generation step with inspectable artifacts.
Emphasize that ceremony is not the goal.
Tie the comparison back to practical review work.
speaker_notes.transition_out: After the method change, show how the runtime surfaces stay aligned.

visual_aid.purpose: Show the compatibility contract in a compact inspectable form.

validation.expected: The table stays readable at 16:9 and keeps all paths visible.
validation.fallback: Move path details to speaker notes if the table becomes too dense.

speaker_notes.key_message: The repo keeps a source skill and two runtime mirrors.
talk_track: Point out that this is a compatibility decision.
Explain that the source skill is edited once, then mirrored.
Use this slide to reduce confusion between Claude Code and Codex paths.

code.caption: Render a validated project to Marp Markdown and standalone HTML.

visual_aid.purpose: Give the audience a concrete command they can run.

validation.expected: The command block renders in a readable mono style.
validation.fallback: Move long command variants into README instead of the slide.

speaker_notes.key_message: The render command validates before it writes output.
talk_track: Call out the intentionally small command surface.
Explain that the renderer runs the ledger and slide-spec checks first.
speaker_notes.transition_out: The command writes HTML first because the browser can inspect it.

slides.title: HTML path comes before PPTX
body: The MVP targets browser-testable HTML first.
The same slide specs can later feed stricter PPTX work.
slides.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[slide-specs.json] --> B[Marp Markdown]
  B --> C[Standalone HTML]
  C --> D[Presenter view]
  C --> E[Browser QA]
  A -. later .-> F[PPTX renderer]

visual_aid.purpose: Show why browser-testable HTML is the first export target.

validation.expected: The Mermaid flow renders as an inspectable diagram without crowding.
validation.fallback: Use a simple static flow if Mermaid rendering is unavailable.

motion.purpose: Use a restrained transition to mark the shift from command to architecture.
motion.reduced_motion_fallback: The slide order communicates the shift without motion.

speaker_notes.key_message: HTML is the fastest path to visual QA and presenter support.
talk_track: Keep this architecture slide focused.
HTML gives fast screenshot and overflow checks.
PPTX remains a later export layer rather than the first renderer.
delivery_cues: Point to Browser QA before mentioning PPTX.

slides.title: Repair with focus
slides.quote: Fix the artifact that failed; keep the rest stable.
slides.no_claims_reason: Closing principle rather than a factual claim.

visual_aid.purpose: Close with a short operating principle instead of a summary paragraph.

speaker_notes.key_message: Targeted repair is the point of the whole pipeline.
talk_track: Close by emphasizing targeted repair.
Invite critique of the artifact sequence rather than the idea of automation in general.
avoid_saying: Do not say the pipeline eliminates human review.
